prompt: What does risk treatment represent in an IRM implementation lifecycle?
choices: Selecting and executing an approved response strategy such as mitigate, transfer, accept, or avoid
Closing all risk records once they are assessed
Converting every risk into an audit finding
Deferring treatment until policy attestation is complete
explanation: Risk treatment operationalizes risk decisions and links them to accountable actions.

prompt: Which factor should most influence whether a risk is mitigated versus accepted?
choices: Residual risk compared with organization risk appetite and control implementation feasibility
Number of dashboards available for the risk domain
Preference of whichever analyst opened the record
Whether the risk has a short description under 100 characters
explanation: Treatment choice is driven by residual exposure versus tolerance and practical control options.

prompt: How should risk treatment plans be connected to enterprise change governance?
choices: Link treatment tasks to approved implementation work and validate control effectiveness after deployment
Run treatment as undocumented emergency work outside change management
Treat risk by creating informational incidents only
Delay treatment updates until quarterly board reporting
explanation: Treatment requires governed implementation and post-change control validation.

prompt: Which status pattern indicates risk treatment execution is healthy?
choices: On-time completion of treatment tasks with measurable reduction in residual risk scores
High volume of overdue treatment tasks with unchanged risk ratings
Frequent reassignment of owners without due dates
Many accepted risks without documented rationale
explanation: Healthy treatment programs show execution discipline and reduced residual exposure.

prompt: What is the best handling strategy when a planned mitigation is delayed by budget constraints?
choices: Document a time-bound risk acceptance with compensating controls and escalation to risk governance
Close the risk as complete because treatment was attempted
Delete the treatment plan and recreate later
Ignore the delay until external audit discovers it
explanation: Delayed mitigations require governed acceptance and compensating safeguards until full treatment resumes.

prompt: Which metric best reflects SIR detection effectiveness?
choices: Mean time to detect from threat occurrence to case creation with quality triage context
Total number of analyst comments per incident
Count of closed incidents without severity classification
Number of notification templates configured
explanation: Detection effectiveness is measured by how quickly and reliably threats become actionable incidents.

prompt: How is mean time to contain (MTTC) used in SIR program management?
choices: Track speed from incident identification to successful containment to evaluate response process efficiency
Measure elapsed time from annual policy review to control attestation
Calculate number of open vulnerabilities grouped by assignment
Estimate how long it takes to archive completed incidents
explanation: MTTC indicates operational containment performance and process bottlenecks.

prompt: Which metric pairing is most useful for evaluating SIR triage quality?
choices: False-positive rate and percentage of incidents correctly prioritized at first assignment
Number of dashboard widgets and report exports
Count of emails sent during major incidents
Volume of historical incidents older than two years
explanation: Good triage should minimize false positives and improve first-pass prioritization accuracy.

prompt: What does repeated growth in incident reopen rates usually indicate?
choices: Containment or eradication actions are incomplete and closure criteria need stronger validation
SOC staffing is oversized for current alert volume
Threat intelligence feeds are too comprehensive
Case templates contain too many optional fields
explanation: High reopen rates often signal weak closure controls or unresolved root issues.

prompt: Which reporting approach best supports executive review of SIR metrics?
choices: Trend MTTD, MTTC, MTTR, severity distribution, and repeat-incident rates with narrative risk context
Show only raw incident counts by month with no segmentation
Report solely on analyst workload by shift
Use one-time screenshots instead of trend dashboards
explanation: Executive metrics should show response performance trends and risk impact context.

prompt: What is the purpose of map suggestions in Service Mapping?
choices: Recommend likely service dependencies from discovery signals to accelerate map creation and validation
Automatically close all map-related tasks without review
Replace CMDB identification rules with manual tags
Disable map health scoring for new services
explanation: Map suggestions speed implementation by proposing dependencies that teams validate.

prompt: How should teams evaluate map suggestions before accepting them?
choices: Validate against known application architecture, traffic patterns, and service owner confirmation
Accept every suggestion to maximize map size quickly
Reject all suggestions and rely only on manual drawing
Approve suggestions based on CI name length
explanation: Suggestions improve speed, but accuracy requires architectural and ownership validation.

prompt: Which condition most improves map suggestion quality over time?
choices: High-quality CMDB data, stable identifiers, and continuous feedback on accepted versus rejected suggestions
Frequent deletion of service maps after each release
Suppressing discovery during maintenance windows permanently
Removing application owners from map governance
explanation: Suggestion accuracy depends on trustworthy data and iterative validation feedback.

prompt: What is the biggest implementation risk when map suggestions are consumed without governance?
choices: Incorrect dependencies can propagate into incident impact, change risk, and outage communication decisions
All service maps become immutable and cannot be edited
MID Servers stop discovering network infrastructure
Event Management no longer creates alerts
explanation: Unchecked suggestions can undermine downstream operational decisions that depend on map accuracy.

prompt: Which KPI best demonstrates effective use of map suggestions?
choices: Reduced time to onboard new services with sustained map accuracy and fewer post-go-live corrections
Higher number of unreviewed suggestions accepted weekly
More duplicate application services in CMDB
Longer delay between discovery runs and map updates
explanation: Effective use balances faster onboarding with reliable topology quality.
